overview: This is an African Moon Moth (Argema mimosae), not a Luna Moth, though they are close cousins. Adults have no functional mouthparts and live for less than a week, relying solely on fat stored during their larval stage. They are found in sub-Saharan Africa and have a wingspan typically measuring 10 to 12 centimeters. The long, trailing tails are believed to help confuse predators, such as bats, by creating false echoes. iNaturalist +3 4 sites African Moon Moth (Argema mimosae) · iNaturalist

This is an African Moon Moth (Argema mimosae), not a Luna Moth, though they are close cousins.
Adults have no functional mouthparts and live for less than a week, relying solely on fat stored during their larval stage.
They are found in sub-Saharan Africa and have a wingspan typically measuring 10 to 12 centimeters.
The long, trailing tails are believed to help confuse predators, such as bats, by creating false echoes.
